chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2670 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُحْرِمُ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ مِنَ اَلرِّيحِ اَلطَّيِّبَةِ وَ لاَ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ مِنَ اَلرِّيحِ اَلْخَبِيثَةِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2670 - Al-Halabi and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A Muhrim may cover his nose from a pleasant fragrance but should not cover his nose from an unpleasant odor."
